text:
CT CHEST / ABDOMEN / PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
RESTAGING EXAMINATION

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, status post prior Whipple procedure (pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy) and adjuvant gemcitabine/capecitabine, now with confirmed hepatic recurrence (segment VI) and bilateral indeterminate pulmonary nodules. Currently on palliative gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel, 3 cycles completed. Restaging to assess treatment response. CA 19-9 at time of scan: 45 U/mL (declining from 67 U/mL at initiation of this regimen). CEA: 3.9 ng/mL.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following administration of 120 mL of iohexol (Omnipaque 350) intravenous contrast, with acquisitions in the portal venous phase. Coronal and sagittal multiplanar reformatted images were generated and reviewed. Lung windows, mediastinal windows, and soft tissue algorithms were applied. Images compared to prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis as well as the dedicated gadoxetate-enhanced MRI liver obtained at the time of recurrence diagnosis.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear without focal consolidation or ground-glass opacity to suggest acute infectious or inflammatory process. The previously noted right lower lobe pulmonary nodule is again identified in the posterior right lower lobe, measuring approximately 7 mm in its greatest dimension, unchanged in size and morphology compared to the prior study. The left lower lobe nodule is similarly unchanged, measuring approximately 5 mm. Neither nodule has developed spiculation, cavitation, or satellite nodules. No new pulmonary nodules are identified above the threshold of detection. The central airways are patent. No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinum is midline. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. Cardiomediastinal silhouette is within normal limits for age. No pericardial effusion.

Chest Wall and Osseous Structures: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion. No rib lesions. Thoracic spine demonstrates mild multilevel degenerative changes without discrete metastatic lesion.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver demonstrates post-surgical morphology consistent with prior pancreaticoduodenectomy and expected compensatory changes. The previously characterized segment VI hepatic lesion measures approximately 1.2 cm in greatest axial dimension, demonstrating subtle peripheral enhancement on portal venous phase imaging, consistent with its prior characterization as metastatic adenocarcinoma on dedicated gadoxetate-enhanced MRI. There is no interval increase in size. No new hypodense, arterially enhancing, or otherwise suspicious hepatic lesions are identified in the remaining hepatic parenchyma. No biliary ductal dilatation. No hepatic abscess or fluid collection.

Gallbladder and Biliary System: The gallbladder is absent, consistent with cholecystectomy performed at the time of Whipple procedure. The common hepatic duct and intrahepatic bile ducts are not dilated. The hepaticojejunostomy anastomotic region appears patent with no perianastomotic fluid collection or biloma.

Pancreas and Post-surgical Anatomy: The pancreatic head and uncinate process are surgically absent, consistent with prior pancreaticoduodenectomy. The residual pancreatic body and tail are visualized and demonstrate expected atrophic changes with parenchymal thinning and mild pancreatic ductal prominence, typical of the post-pancreatectomy state and consistent with exocrine insufficiency. The pancreaticojejunostomy anastomosis is intact without evidence of leak or peripancreatic fluid collection. No new solid or cystic lesion in the pancreatic remnant. The mesenteric root shows expected post-surgical fibrosis without discrete soft tissue mass to suggest local tumor recurrence in the pancreatic bed.

Spleen: The spleen is normal in size and attenuation without focal lesion.

Adrenal Glands: Both adrenal glands are within normal limits in size and configuration. No adrenal mass.

Kidneys and Ureters: Both kidneys are normal in size and enhancement without focal cortical lesion, hydronephrosis, or urothelial thickening. No nephrolithiasis. The ureters are not dilated.

Stomach and Small Bowel: The gastrojejunostomy is identified in the expected location. The afferent and efferent jejunal limbs are patent without obstruction or distension. No bowel wall thickening. No pneumatosis. No free air to suggest perforation.

Colon: The visualized colon appears unremarkable without wall thickening, mass, or obstruction.

Retroperitoneum and Mesentery: The aorta is normal in caliber. No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. The superior mesenteric artery and superior mesenteric vein are widely patent without encasement or thrombosis. No discrete soft tissue nodule in the retroperitoneum or mesentery to suggest lymph node metastasis or implant.

Peritoneum: No ascites. No peritoneal nodularity or omental thickening to suggest peritoneal carcinomatosis. The peritoneal surfaces appear smooth.

---

PELVIS:

Bladder: Urinary bladder is partially distended and unremarkable.

Female Pelvic Organs: The uterus and bilateral adnexa are atrophic, consistent with the patient's age and postmenopausal status. No adnexal mass or pelvic free fluid.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes: No pelvic lymphadenopathy.

Osseous Pelvis: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic bony lesion. Mild degenerative changes of the sacroiliac joints and hips.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. The known segment VI hepatic metastasis remains stable in size at approximately 1.2 cm, without interval enlargement, new internal characteristics, or development of satellite lesions. No additional hepatic lesions are identified.

2. The two previously noted pulmonary nodules — right lower lobe (7 mm) and left lower lobe (5 mm) — are unchanged in size, morphology, and number. No new pulmonary nodules are detected.

3. No new sites of metastatic disease identified. No peritoneal implants. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes in any regional chain surveyed.

4. Expected post-pancreaticoduodenectomy anatomy without evidence of local recurrence in the surgical bed or along the mesenteric root.

5. Overall, the imaging findings taken together with the declining CA 19-9 (67 → 45 U/mL) are consistent with disease that is holding steady in response to the current palliative regimen. No interval progression is demonstrated on this study.
